$comment: Source registry for mcc.vesperio.ai ingestion agent. status: 'verified' = URL/feed confirmed working; 'unverified' = drafted from knowledge, must be checked before first run; 'dead' after 3 consecutive failures. tier is legacy metadata (1 = primary-leaning, 2 = press); the SNR model classes each attached source per claim (see CLAUDE.md 'The SNR score' and prompts/update-items.md). Signal-tier sourcing is governed by signals.json, not this file.

eo_operators.name: Planet Labs
eo_operators.notes: Also see SEC filings (PL). Florian's employer: include for completeness, apply no insider context ever. | [2026-07-05] Fetched cleanly; most recent Pulse post (June 17) predates this run's freshness window, no candidate. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent Pulse post still June 17, 2026, outside the freshness window, no candidate. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent Pulse post still June 17, 2026 (a later April item also visible), nothing inside the freshness window. | [2026-07-05] Fetched cleanly; June Pulse posts (Jun 17, 24, 25) are thought-leadership/product-blog content, not discrete news events, so none used as a candidate this run. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched Pulse cleanly; most recent post still June 25, 2026, well outside this run's freshness window (last sweep 2026-07-05 08:58 UTC). No candidate. | [2026-07-06] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent Pulse post still June 25, 2026, outside this run's freshness window (last sweep 2026-07-05 18:12 UTC). No candidate.

eo_operators.name: ICEYE
eo_operators.notes: [2026-07-05] Fetched cleanly; six press releases visible, two within freshness window used as candidates. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; same six press releases as the prior run (the two newest already in items.json), nothing new within the freshness window. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; same six press releases as the last two runs, most recent still July 1, 2026 (already in items.json), no new candidate. | [2026-07-05] Fetched cleanly; four press releases within the 30-day backfill window (Jun 15, 17, 18, 22) found beyond the two already in items.json. Three used (Liberty wildfire insurance, Portugal SAR satellites, Norway NVE); the June 22 Australian Space Award win was excluded as too minor to clear the notable bar. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; same six press releases as prior runs, most recent still July 1, 2026 (already in items.json). No new candidate. | [2026-07-06] Re-fetched cleanly (301 redirect followed); same press releases as prior runs, most recent still July 1, 2026 (already in items.json). No new candidate.

eo_operators.name: Umbra
eo_operators.notes: Umbra publishes unusually openly (pricing, tasking). High signal.

eo_operators.name: Spire Global
eo_operators.notes: RF/weather, adjacent to EO. Also SEC (SPIR).

launch_providers.name: SpaceX
launch_providers.notes: Sparse official comms. Launch cadence better tracked via Launch Library API. | [2026-07-05] Standard fetch returned only the page header (JS-rendered, no article content) on two attempts; needs a JS-capable fetch or an alternate feed. | [2026-07-05] Fetched again (both WebFetch and curl with descriptive User-Agent); still only the Angular app shell with no article markup, no content extractable. One more consecutive failure flips this to dead. | [2026-07-05] Third consecutive failure. Both WebFetch and curl with a descriptive User-Agent return only the Angular app shell (no article markup); flipping to dead per the 3-failure rule. Re-verify manually if the site changes to a server-rendered news page.

launch_providers.name: Rocket Lab
launch_providers.notes: Also SEC (RKLB). Domain changed from rocketlabusa.com in 2025, verify. | [2026-07-05] HTTP 403 Forbidden on two attempts; site may be blocking the fetch tool's user agent. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing page now fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent (real headlines and dates), reversing the prior 403. Individual article pages, however, are gated by a Cloudflare JS challenge (403 via both WebFetch and curl) -- listing metadata alone isn't enough to verify facts for publication; see held item. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing page fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent (same behavior as last run). Individual article pages are still gated by a Cloudflare JS challenge, but the Rocket Lab/Iridium deal referenced in the listing was independently confirmed this run via Rocket Lab's own 8-K exhibit on SEC EDGAR, so the listing-vs-article gap no longer blocks that story. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing again fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent and lists the VICTUS HAZE Puma and Synspective StriX-10 launches, but individual article pages remain Cloudflare-gated (same as prior runs). VICTUS HAZE was independently confirmed via Launch Library's mission description instead. | [2026-07-05] The /updates/ listing fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent; most recent entry still the July 3 Iridium acquisition post (already published from the RKLB 8-K). No new dated entries since. | [2026-07-06] The /updates/ listing fetches cleanly via curl with a descriptive User-Agent; most recent entry still the July 3 Iridium acquisition post (already published). No new dated entries.

launch_providers.name: Arianespace
launch_providers.notes: FR originals sometimes richer than EN.

launch_providers.name: Blue Origin
launch_providers.notes: New Glenn ramp relevant to EO rideshare economics.

launch_providers.name: ISRO
launch_providers.notes: PSLV/SSLV rideshare pricing pressure is a recurring story.

launch_providers.name: Isar Aerospace
launch_providers.notes: Spectrum program; key EU small-launch story.

launch_tracking.name: Launch Library 2 (TheSpaceDevs)
launch_tracking.notes: Free API, rate-limited (~15 req/hr unauth). The backbone for launch events. Verify current API version. | [2026-07-05] Fetched previous/upcoming launches directly; no SpaceX or Rocket Lab launch within the freshness window carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine manifest. | [2026-07-05] One previous + one upcoming call; no SpaceX or Rocket Lab launch in the window carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine manifest. | [2026-07-05] One previous + one upcoming call (15 results each), consistent with the rate-limit lesson. SpaceX and Rocket Lab entries are routine Starlink/Electron manifest launches; none carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine cadence already on record. | [2026-07-05] One previous-launches call plus one direct per-launch fetch for VICTUS HAZE; used to confirm launch occurrence facts for VICTUS HAZE (confirmed tier) and to cross-check the Atlas V/Amazon Leo mission. | [2026-07-05] One previous-launches call (15 results). The one launch inside the freshness window (Falcon 9 Starlink Group 10-50, 2026-07-05 10:50 UTC) is routine cadence with no notable angle beyond record; no candidate. | [2026-07-06] One previous-launches call (2.3.0, 15 results) plus one upcoming call; no launch inside the freshness window (last sweep 2026-07-05 18:12 UTC) carried a notable, sourced angle beyond the routine manifest already on record.

launch_tracking.name: Gunter's Space Page
launch_tracking.notes: Best free satellite/payload encyclopedia. Registry reference source, never a news basis. Terms: summarization/RAG only with clear attribution and a direct link to the exact page; cite deep-link URLs in registry source fields, never sum counts across pages.

regulatory.name: FCC IBFS (satellite filings)
regulatory.notes: fcc.report is an unofficial but scrapeable mirror; official IBFS search is licensing.fcc.gov and painful. Constellation modifications show up here months before press releases. Differentiator source.

regulatory.name: ITU Space Network filings (SNL)
regulatory.notes: Clunky. Treat as phase-2 hardening target, not day-one.

regulatory.name: NOAA CRSRA (US remote sensing licenses)
regulatory.notes: License grants/modifications reveal US EO operator capability plans early (resolution, NEI waivers). URL may have moved; confirm during recon.

financial.name: SEC EDGAR 8-K feed: PL
financial.notes: EDGAR atom feeds are reliable and free. Requires User-Agent header. One entry per ticker. | [2026-07-05] 403 without a User-Agent header, succeeds with one set (per existing note); most recent PL 8-K filed 2026-06-05, outside freshness window. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still 2026-06-05, outside the freshness window. | [2026-07-05] Most recent filing (2026-06-05, items 1.01/9.01) covers a $1.5B ATM equity distribution agreement, used as the confirmed-tier basis for this run's Planet Labs item. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still the 2026-06-05 ATM offering 8-K (already published). No new filing. | [2026-07-06] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still the 2026-06-05 ATM offering 8-K (already published). No new filing.

financial.name: SEC EDGAR 8-K feed: BKSY
financial.notes: [2026-07-05] First successful fetch this run. Most recent filing is 2026-05-22, outside the 30-day backfill window; no candidate. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still 2026-05-22. No new filing. | [2026-07-06] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still 2026-05-22. No new filing.

financial.name: SEC EDGAR 8-K feed: RKLB
financial.notes: [2026-07-05] First successful fetch (curl with a descriptive User-Agent, no 403). Most recent filing is a June 29, 2026 8-K (items 1.01/7.01/9.01) containing the Rocket Lab-Iridium merger agreement and joint press release, used as the primary source for a confirmed item this run. | [2026-07-05] Refetched cleanly. The 2026-06-05 filing (item 5.02) is a routine Chief Accounting Officer appointment, below the notability bar; no candidate beyond the already-published Iridium acquisition. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still the 2026-06-29 Iridium merger 8-K (already published). No new filing. | [2026-07-06] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still the 2026-06-29 Iridium merger 8-K (already published). No new filing.

No new filing. | [2026-07-06] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still the 2026-06-08 CFO transition 8-K, below the notability bar. No new filing.

The 2026-06-15 filing (item 5.07) is routine annual-meeting voting results; no candidate. | [2026-07-05] Re-fetched cleanly; most recent filing still the 2026-06-15 routine annual-meeting voting-results 8-K. procurement.name: SAM.gov (space/EO NAICS filters)
procurement.notes: Free API with key. Filter by NAICS 517410/336414 and keyword sets. Phase in after core news loop works.

procurement.name: NGA / NRO public announcements
procurement.notes: Commercial imagery contract news (EOCL-type). High relevance to EO revenue picture.

non_english.name: CASC news (China Aerospace)
non_english.notes: EN version thin; zh original at spacechina.com richer. Chinese launch cadence is the biggest blind spot in Western feeds. Differentiator.

non_english.name: CGSTL / Chang Guang
non_english.notes: Jilin-1 constellation. Validated as OSINT-rich during planning research. WeChat is the richer channel but not machine-accessible; treat site-only for v1.

non_english.name: CNES press (FR)
non_english.notes: You read FR natively; agent handles it fine too.

non_english.name: Xinhua tech/space (EN)
non_english.notes: Fallback when CGSTL/CASC sites are unreachable from outside China. State media: label origin on items, treat launch facts as reliable, framing as not.

aggregators_crosscheck.name: SpaceNews
aggregators_crosscheck.notes: Discovery + cross-check only. Never republish their reporting as an item basis; find the primary source they cite. | [2026-07-05] First successful fetch of the RSS feed. Discovery/cross-check only per source notes; no SpaceX, Rocket Lab, ICEYE, Planet Labs, or Iridium story in the feed added information beyond what was already sourced directly from primary sources this run. | [2026-07-05] Fetched cleanly; used as reported-tier basis for several financial, regulatory, human-spaceflight, and launch candidates this run since their own primary sources (NASA, FCC, ULA, Amazon, Vantor, Blue Origin, Firefly, SSC Space) are outside this run's filtered source list. | [2026-07-05] RSS feed fetched cleanly. One entry inside the freshness window (Airbus/ESA Aeolus-2 development contract, July 5 15:17 UTC) evaluated and excluded as out of scope: a legacy ESA/Eumetsat institutional weather-science procurement with Airbus as heritage prime, not a new-space-economy event. See SWEEP_MEMORY for the judgment call. | [2026-07-06] RSS feed fetched cleanly. One entry inside the freshness window (NASA/SBA strategic capital partnership, July 6 01:43 UTC) used as the reported-tier basis for a new item; the Airbus/Aeolus-2 entry (July 5 15:17 UTC) predates the window and was already excluded last run.

aggregators_crosscheck.name: European Spaceflight
aggregators_crosscheck.notes: Best coverage of EU launcher startups (Isar, RFA, MaiaSpace, PLD). | [2026-07-05] First fetch of this source's RSS feed; succeeded cleanly. Used for the OHB capital raise and Isar Aerospace/Planet Germany launch deal. | [2026-07-05] RSS feed fetched cleanly; most recent entry (Isar Aerospace/Planet Germany, July 2 15:18 UTC) predates this run's freshness window. No candidate. | [2026-07-06] RSS feed fetched cleanly; most recent entry (Isar Aerospace/Planet Germany, July 2 15:18 UTC) predates this run's freshness window. No candidate.
